speaker:
WELSH
               
              CAPTAIN
text: My Lord of Salisbury, we have stayed ten days

speaker:
WELSH
               
              CAPTAIN
text: And hardly kept our countrymen together,

speaker:
WELSH
               
              CAPTAIN
text: And yet we hear no tidings from the King.

speaker:
WELSH
               
              CAPTAIN
text: Therefore we will disperse ourselves. Farewell.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Stay yet another day, thou trusty Welshman.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: The King reposeth all his confidence in thee.

speaker:
WELSH
               
              CAPTAIN
text: ’Tis thought the King is dead. We will not stay.

speaker:
WELSH
               
              CAPTAIN
text: The bay trees in our country are all withered,

speaker:
WELSH
               
              CAPTAIN
text: And meteors fright the fixèd stars of heaven;

speaker:
WELSH
               
              CAPTAIN
text: The pale-faced moon looks bloody on the Earth,

speaker:
WELSH
               
              CAPTAIN
text: And lean-looked prophets whisper fearful change;

speaker:
WELSH
               
              CAPTAIN
text: Rich men look sad, and ruffians dance and leap,

speaker:
WELSH
               
              CAPTAIN
text: The one in fear to lose what they enjoy,

speaker:
WELSH
               
              CAPTAIN
text: The other to enjoy by rage and war.

speaker:
WELSH
               
              CAPTAIN
text: These signs forerun the death or fall of kings.

speaker:
WELSH
               
              CAPTAIN
text: Farewell. Our countrymen are gone and fled,

speaker:
WELSH
               
              CAPTAIN
text: As well assured Richard their king is dead.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Ah, Richard! With the eyes of heavy mind

speaker: SALISBURY
text: I see thy glory like a shooting star

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Fall to the base earth from the firmament.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Thy sun sets weeping in the lowly west,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Witnessing storms to come, woe, and unrest.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Thy friends are fled to wait upon thy foes,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And crossly to thy good all fortune goes.
